name: Tainan (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace, and the cultural heritage of Taiwan's oldest city
the warmth of the south and the spirit of the city's people

description: A white field with a stylised landscape composition: dark-grey mountains in the upper hoist, gold and red coastal elements with a stylised fish or bird emblem, and a red wave-and-sun motif.
history: Tainan, Taiwan's oldest city and former capital under the Kingdom of Tungning (1661–1683) and the Qing administration, was promoted to a special municipality on 25 December 2010 by merging the original Tainan City with Tainan County. The current city flag was adopted at that time. The composition layers historic and natural elements: mountains, coast, sun and the migratory black-faced spoonbill that winters in the city's wetlands.

features.role: Tainan city emblem — landscape with mountains, sea, sun and bird
features.symbolism: layered composition representing Tainan's mountains (dark grey), the southern sun and red sea (red and yellow), and the black-faced spoonbill that winters in the city's wetlands; together they express Tainan's identity as Taiwan's historic and ecological heart
